content.problemStatement:
Monisha likes to paint. She has painted NNN paintings (numbered 111 through NNN) and wants to choose some subset of these paintings for an exhibition. For each valid iii, the iii-th painting has beauty bib_ibi‚Äč and the probability that it will be displayed at the exhibition is pip_ipi‚Äč. Each painting is chosen or excluded from the exhibition independently randomly.

The beauty of the resulting exhibition is the bitwise XOR of the beauties of all displayed paintings. If no paintings are displayed, the XOR is 000.

Monisha wants to find out how beautiful her exhibition is going to be. Help her compute the expected value of the beauty of the exhibition.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated real numbers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč. Each of these numbers is given with at most five digits after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number ‚ÄĒ the expected beauty of the exhibition. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The first painting must be displayed at the exhibition, the second one must be excluded. If the third painting is displayed (with probability 0.50.50.5), the beauty is 5‚äē2=75 \oplus 2 = 75‚äē2=7; if it is excluded (with probability 0.50.50.5), the beauty is 555. The expected beauty is 0.5‚čÖ5+0.5‚čÖ7=60.5 \cdot 5 + 0.5 \cdot 7 = 60.5‚čÖ5+0.5‚čÖ7=6.
Example case 2: If there is an odd number of paintings at the exhibition, the beauty is 222; this has a probability of 0.50.50.5. If there is an even number of paintings, the beauty is 000 (with probability 0.50.50.5), so the expected beauty is 0.5‚čÖ0+0.5‚čÖ2=10.5 \cdot 0 + 0.5 \cdot 2 = 10.5‚čÖ0+0.5‚čÖ2=1.
